title: Markdown rendering pipeline with syntax highlighting
purpose: Assistant text arrives as raw markdown via TextDelta events. This brief transforms it into styled terminal output with syntax-highlighted code blocks. Without this, assistant output is plain text with no visual structure — no bold, no headers, no coloured code.
task: Implement the markdown rendering pipeline in render/markdown.rs using pulldown-cmark as the event-based parser. Map markdown elements to ANSI-styled output via the NT-002 style system: bold, italic (with underline fallback), headers, inline code, lists, horizontal rules, links (OSC 8 or bracketed). Implement syntect integration in render/syntax.rs for fenced code blocks: load the compressed grammar set, highlight using the language hint, fall back to find_syntax_by_first_line for unlabeled blocks. Implement the streaming buffer strategy: incomplete inline spans (bold/italic markers) buffer until the closing marker arrives; code fences buffer from opening to closing marker then render the complete highlighted block. Plain text outside any open marker flushes immediately.

requirements.title: pulldown-cmark parser integration
requirements.spec: The markdown renderer SHALL parse input text using pulldown-cmark's event-based streaming parser AND produce styled terminal output.
acceptance: MarkdownRenderer struct accepts text chunks and produces styled output strings
Uses pulldown-cmark's Parser with default options
Output strings contain ANSI escape sequences for styling
Test: input '**bold**' produces output containing bold SGR

requirements.title: Inline formatting — bold, italic, inline code
requirements.spec: The renderer SHALL render bold text with ANSI bold, italic with ANSI italic (underline fallback), and inline code with a distinct foreground colour.
acceptance: **text** renders with bold SGR attribute
*text* renders with italic SGR when italic_support is true, underline when false
`code` renders with distinct foreground colour from the style system
Nested formatting works: ***bold italic*** renders with both attributes
Test: italic text on a terminal without italic support renders with underline

requirements.title: Headers
requirements.spec: The renderer SHALL render headers with bold attribute and size-appropriate formatting.
acceptance: # H1 renders bold with a blank line before
## H2 renders bold
### H3 and deeper render bold
Headers are visually distinct from body text
Test: '# Title' input produces bold-styled output

requirements.title: Lists, horizontal rules, links
requirements.spec: The renderer SHALL render bullet and numbered lists with indentation, horizontal rules as terminal-width lines, and links as OSC 8 hyperlinks or bracketed text.
acceptance: Unordered lists render with bullet characters and 2-space indentation per level
Ordered lists render with sequential numbers and 2-space indentation per level
Horizontal rules (---) render as a ─── line spanning the terminal width
[text](url) renders as OSC 8 hyperlink when supported, 'text (url)' otherwise
Test: nested list input produces correctly indented output
Test: link renders as bracketed text when osc_hyperlinks is false

requirements.title: Syntect integration for code blocks
requirements.spec: WHEN a fenced code block has a language hint, THE SYSTEM SHALL syntax-highlight the block using syntect with the matching grammar AND SHALL fall back to find_syntax_by_first_line for unlabeled blocks.
acceptance: SyntaxHighlighter struct wraps syntect's SyntaxSet and ThemeSet
Grammar set loaded from syntect's compressed binary dump (~100 languages)
highlight(code, language) returns ANSI-styled output using the language grammar
When language is None or unrecognised, falls back to find_syntax_by_first_line
When no syntax is detected, renders as plain monospace (no highlighting)
Colour output adapts to TerminalCaps (true colour palette vs 256-colour mapping)
Test: Rust code block produces output with distinct colour for keywords

requirements.title: Code fence buffering
requirements.spec: WHEN the renderer encounters an opening code fence (```) in streaming input, THE SYSTEM SHALL buffer all content until the closing fence arrives, THEN render the complete block with syntax highlighting.
acceptance: Opening ``` triggers buffer mode — subsequent text is accumulated, not flushed
Closing ``` triggers flush: accumulated content is highlighted and written to output
Language hint on the opening fence (```rust) is captured and passed to the highlighter
If the message ends without a closing fence, the buffered content is flushed as plain text
Test: streaming input with ``` on one chunk and closing ``` on a later chunk produces a single highlighted block

requirements.title: Inline span buffering
requirements.spec: WHEN the renderer encounters an opening inline marker (** or *) without its closing marker in the same chunk, THE SYSTEM SHALL buffer content until the closing marker arrives, THEN render the complete styled span.
acceptance: Opening ** without closing ** in the same chunk triggers inline buffer mode
Subsequent chunks are accumulated until ** is found, then the complete bold span is rendered
Same behaviour for * (italic) markers
Plain text before an opening marker is flushed immediately — only the open span is buffered
If no closing marker arrives within a configurable limit (e.g. 10 chunks), the buffer is flushed as plain text to avoid indefinite buffering
Test: '**bold' in one chunk and ' text**' in the next produces a single bold span

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the streaming event loop or ProviderEvent dispatch — that is NT-011
SHALL NOT implement per-tool rendering — that is NT-006/NT-007
SHALL NOT handle ToolCallDelta events — the markdown renderer processes text content only
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: Bold, italic, headers, inline code, lists, horizontal rules, and links render correctly — verified by tests
Fenced code blocks with language hints produce syntax-highlighted output — verified by test
Streaming buffer handles split markers across chunks — verified by tests
256-colour fallback produces valid output — verified by test with true_colour=false
